title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin compares current coding agents to a genius with amnesia who can only see a tiny corner of a codebase and forgets between interactions. He argues that context limitations and memory loss severely restrict agent value regardless of underlying capability, and positions Nx and Polygraph as solutions to maximize agent autonomy.
writeup: Victor Savkin of Nx frames current coding agents as a genius with amnesia: capable in principle, but limited to a tiny corner of a codebase and forgetful between interactions. The argument is that context limitations and memory loss, not raw model capability, are what cap an agent's value on real codebases. The talk positions Nx and Polygraph as approaches to context management and persistent memory that aim to widen what an agent can see and remember, increasing agent autonomy.
